mechanism_narrative: Quarterly growth in real GDP consistently predicts positive S&P 500 returns over the subsequent month because expanding aggregate demand improves corporate earnings expectations and reduces equity risk premiums.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in quarters with positive GDP growth are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A decline in the unemployment rate below 5.0 percent signals a tightening labor market that boosts consumer spending power and corporate revenue growth, driving positive equity returns over the next month.
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 20-day returns in more than 60 percent of instances where UNRATE drops below 5.0 percent, or the signal shows no predictive power after controlling for FEDFUNDS changes.

mechanism_narrative: The joint behavior of falling federal funds rates and declining consumer price inflation compresses real discount rates while stabilizing input costs, creating a favorable environment for equity valuation expansion over a quarterly horizon.
failure_condition: Equity returns are negative or flat when both FEDFUNDS and CPIAUCSL decline simultaneously over a 60-day window, or the interaction term in a multivariate regression yields a coefficient with p > 0.10.

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production indicates expanding manufacturing capacity and supply chain normalization, which directly supports industrial sector earnings and lifts the broader market index over a one-week horizon.
failure_condition: Positive month-over-month changes in INDPRO consistently precede negative or flat S&P 500 returns over the following 5 trading days, or the signal fails to outperform a random baseline in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained increases in housing starts reflect improving household balance sheets and consumer confidence, which translate into higher durable goods spending and positive equity returns over a quarterly period.
failure_condition: HOUST rises for three consecutive months while SPX 60-day forward returns remain negative, or the correlation between HOUST growth and equity returns drops below 0.10 over a 5-year window.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp contraction in residential investment spending signals weakening household wealth and reduced consumption demand, which depresses corporate earnings forecasts and drives negative equity returns over the next month.
candidate_trigger: RSAFS declines more than 5 percent over three months
failure_condition: SPX 20-day returns are positive in more than 65 percent of cases following a 5 percent drop in RSAFS, or the signal shows no incremental predictive power beyond UNRATE movements.

mechanism_narrative: The simultaneous rise in total nonfarm payrolls and a falling unemployment rate confirms robust labor market recovery, which sustains wage growth and consumer demand, driving positive equity returns over a monthly horizon.
failure_condition: Equity returns are negative when PAYEMS increases and UNRATE decreases in the same month, or the joint signal fails to produce a Rank IC above 0.05 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Rapid increases in the federal funds rate during an aggressive tightening cycle raise borrowing costs and compress equity valuations, leading to negative S&P 500 returns over a one-week horizon as discount rates rise faster than earnings growth.
failure_condition: SPX 5-day returns are positive in more than 60 percent of instances where FEDFUNDS rises by more than 25 basis points in a single month, or the signal shows no predictive power after controlling for INDPRO changes.
